descript.abstract: This feature class/shapefile contains locations of Hospitals for 50 US states, Washington D.C., US territories of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, Northern Mariana Islands, Palau, and Virgin Islands. The dataset only includes hospital facilities based on data acquired from various state departments or federal sources which has been referenced in the SOURCE field. Hospital facilities which do not occur in these sources will be not present in the database. The source data was available in a variety of formats (pdfs, tables, webpages, etc.) which was cleaned and geocoded and then converted into a spatial database. The database does not contain nursing homes or health centers. Hospitals have been categorized into children, chronic disease, critical access, general acute care, long term care, military, psychiatric, rehabilitation, special, and women based on the range of the available values from the various sources after removing similarities.
descript.purpose: This feature class/shapefile contains Hospitals derived from various sources (refer SOURCE field) for the Homeland Infrastructure Foundation-Level Data (HIFLD) database. (https://gii.dhs.gov/HIFLD)
descript.supplinf: This feature class/shapefile has a one-to-many relationship class (HospitalsToTrauma)/relate with the “Trauma_Levels” table. This table captures the relationship between Hospitals and the state trauma level designations. “Hospitals” is the origin using STATE as the primary key. “Trauma_Levels” table is the destination using STATE as the foreign key.

timeperd.current: Time period date range corresponds to the data range covered by the SOURCEDATE field

idinfo.useconst: None (Public Use). Users are advised to read the data set's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations.
idinfo.datacred: Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL); Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Geospatial Management Office (GMO) .

attracc.attraccr: No formal attribute accuracy test was conducted. Data from the authoritative sources (as identified in other sections of this metadata) is assumed to be correct and accurate. See also “Overview Description” in the “Entities and Attributes” metadata section.

dataqual.logic: Data were extracted from sources (identified in the SOURCE field) and other open source websites to populate the attribute fields. For string and date data types, NOT AVAILABLE values donate missing data that was not present in the source data. For numeric data types, -999 denotes missing data that was not present in the source data. During quality control, attributes were checked to ensure that they fell within the expected ranges or had expected/valid values. Text attribute values were checked for spelling and usage consistency. No topology tests were conducted. All records were verified to occur within the spatial domain of the feature class/shapefile.
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of the most recent date indicated in the Time Period section.

horizpa.horizpar: None; all efforts are made to place the geographic representation of the facility on-entity using the best available imagery from a wide variety of sources. Users should read and understand the VAL_METHOD metadata as well as the Processing Steps.

vertacc.vertaccr: None; dataset contains no vertical data.

procstep.procdesc: Authoritative data sources are identified through open source research. That data is then acquired and transformed into a usable data format. The properly formatted data then goes through preprocessing steps that includes a geocoding operation to give spatial context to the data. The data goes through further refinement using remote sensing techniques. After preprocessing, every record goes through a review process and each point is moved to a facility based on-entity location based on interpretation of the best available imagery and open source information. Hospitals with multi-building campus were located on a large building towards the center of campus. The VAL_DATE field indicates the date in which the review step took place.

enttyp.enttypd: Attribute fields were populated using sources identified in the SOURCE field for the period covered in the Time Period metadata and other open source data. For string and date data types, NOT AVAILABLE values denote missing data that was not present in the source data. For numeric data types, -999 denotes missing data that was not present in the source data. However, the missing data may be present in other sources not yet discovered and used in the creation and/or maintenance of this dataset. These records may be populated with valid information in future update cycles.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
Unique ID for each feature. Unique feature ID values are unique to this dataset ONLY and may have changed from the previous versions of the dataset, and/or change in a future version of the dataset.
Feature name.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: Primary street address and/or P.O. Box, Mail Stop and/or location description (i.e. 5th Avenue and Main Street).
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: City name.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: State abbreviation (United States and its Territories).
Zip code (5 digit).
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: Extended zip code (4 digits).
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: General telephone number in the following format or reformatted from the SOURCE to: (xxx) xxx-xxxx EXT xxx.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: The type of hospitals defined by each individual state which have been grouped into 10 common types based on the range of values after removing similarities.

edom.edomvd: A hospital with a majority of its inpatients under 18 years.
A hospital that specializes in providing health care for chronic diseases (asthma, cancer, ALS etc.).
A facility that provides outpatient services, as well as inpatient services on a limited basis, to people in rural areas.
A hospital that provides inpatient medical care and other related services for acute medical conditions or injuries (usually for a short-term illness or condition).
Hospitals which focus on patients who, on average, stay more than 25 days.
A hospital which is managed by the active military (Air Force, Army, or Navy) or by the Department of Veterans Affairs.
A hospital that provides inpatient psychiatric services for the diagnosis and treatment of mental illness.
A hospital that specializes in improving or restoring a patient's functional ability through therapies.
A hospital that specializes in providing health care for specific type of diseases.
A hospital that specializes in providing health care for women and infants.
Please see “Entity Type Definition” section.

attr.attrdef: Operational status of the feature.

edom.edomvd: Operational.
Not operational.
Please see "Entity Type Definition" section.

attr.attrdef: Sum of TTL_STAFF and BEDS field. For this version population is total number of beds.
United States and its Territories county name and legal/statistical area description (county, borough, parish, municipality, census area).
US County Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) Code (5 digit)

codesetd.codesetn: 2010 FIPS Codes for Counties and County Equivalent Entities

attr.attrdef: Country Code (3 character).

codesetd.codesetn: Geopolitical Entities, Names, and Codes (GENC) Standard
codesetd.codesets: National System for Geospatial Intelligence (NSG) Standards Registry (https://nsgreg.nga.mil/genc/discovery)

attr.attrdef: The angular distance north or south from the equator on the earth’s surface.
The angular distance east or west from the prime meridian on the earth’s surface.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code is the standard used by Federal statistical agencies in classifying business establishments for the purpose of collecting, analyzing, and publishing statistical data related to the U.S. business economy.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) definition is the standard used by Federal statistical agencies in classifying business establishments for the purpose of collecting, analyzing, and publishing statistical data related to the U.S. business economy.
Original source used to identify geospatial location and/or attribution for the feature.
Date of the source, or, if not available, the date ORNL accessed the source.
Methodology used to validate geospatial location of the feature.

edom.edomvd: Street address geocoding used to determine the location.
The best available imagery used to determine the location.
Imagery and supplemental open source information used to determine the location.
Feature could not be located or the feature has not yet been validated.

attr.attrdef: Date when geospatial location was last validated for the feature.
The feature specific website.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: Feature ID maintained by individual state.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: Any other name for the feature present in data.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: US State FIPS Code (2 digits).

codesetd.codesetn: 2010 FIPS Codes for Counties and County Equivalent Entities.

attr.attrdef: Type of owner of the feature (Private, Federal, VA, etc.)

edom.edomvd: Hospital owned by a district or an authority
Hospital owned by the federal government.
Hospital owned by a local government entity like a city or county.
Hospital owned by a state.
Hospital owned by a voluntary non-profit like a church.
Please see "Entity Type Definition" section
Hospital owned by a private entity.

attr.attrdef: Total number of personnel working at the hospital. Not populated in current version.
Total number of beds available.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: The trauma level(s) for the hospital as provided by each state.
attr.attrdefs: The responsible organization as identified in the SOURCE field.

attr.attrdef: Y or N indicating on the presence or absence of a helipad.

edom.edomvd: Helipad Absent
Please see "Entity Type Definition" section
Helipad Present

overview.eaover: The entity and attribute information provided here describes the tabular data associated with the data set. Please review the detailed descriptions that are provided (the individual attribute descriptions) for information on the values that appear as fields/table entries of the data set. Please see the “Processing Steps” section for further methodology .Some features within this dataset were collected using image processing. For string and date data types, NOT AVAILABLE values donate missing data that was not present in the source data. For numeric data types, -999 denotes missing data that was not present in the source data. However, the missing data may be present in other sources not yet discovered and used in the creation and/or maintenance of this dataset. These records may be populated with valid information in future update cycles.

distinfo.distliab: Distributor assumes no liability for misuse of data.

stdorder.fees: None. No fees are applicable for obtaining the data set.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata

lineage.Process: ExportFeatures Hospitals C:\Mac\Home\Desktop\Hospitals\Hospital_Data.shp # NOT_USE_ALIAS "ID "ID" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,ID,0,49;NAME "NAME" true true false 155 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,NAME,0,154;ADDRESS "ADDRESS" true true false 80 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,ADDRESS,0,79;CITY "CITY" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,CITY,0,49;STATE "STATE" true true false 2 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,STATE,0,1;ZIP "ZIP" true true false 15 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,ZIP,0,14;ZIP4 "ZIP4" true true false 15 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,ZIP4,0,14;TELEPHONE "TELEPHONE" true true false 30 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,TELEPHONE,0,29;TYPE "TYPE" true true false 20 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,TYPE,0,19;STATUS "STATUS" true true false 10 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,STATUS,0,9;POPULATION "POPULATION" true true false 10 Long 0 10,First,#,Hospitals,POPULATION,-1,-1;COUNTY "COUNTY" true true false 30 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,COUNTY,0,29;COUNTYFIPS "COUNTYFIPS" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,COUNTYFIPS,0,49;COUNTRY "COUNTRY" true true false 3 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,COUNTRY,0,2;LATITUDE "LATITUDE" true true false 19 Double 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,LATITUDE,-1,-1;LONGITUDE "LONGITUDE" true true false 19 Double 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,LONGITUDE,-1,-1;NAICS_CODE "NAICS_CODE" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,NAICS_CODE,0,49;NAICS_DESC "NAICS_DESC" true true false 254 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,NAICS_DESC,0,253;SOURCE "SOURCE" true true false 254 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,SOURCE,0,253;SOURCEDATE "SOURCEDATE" true true false 8 Date 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,SOURCEDATE,-1,-1;VAL_METHOD "VAL_METHOD" true true false 30 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,VAL_METHOD,0,29;VAL_DATE "VAL_DATE" true true false 8 Date 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,VAL_DATE,-1,-1;WEBSITE "WEBSITE" true true false 254 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,WEBSITE,0,253;STATE_ID "STATE_ID" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,STATE_ID,0,49;ALT_NAME "ALT_NAME" true true false 254 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,ALT_NAME,0,253;ST_FIPS "ST_FIPS" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,ST_FIPS,0,49;OWNER "OWNER" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,OWNER,0,49;TTL_STAFF "TTL_STAFF" true true false 10 Long 0 10,First,#,Hospitals,TTL_STAFF,-1,-1;BEDS "BEDS" true true false 19 Double 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,BEDS,-1,-1;TRAUMA "TRAUMA" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,TRAUMA,0,49;HELIPAD "HELIPAD" true true false 50 Text 0 0,First,#,Hospitals,HELIPAD,0,49" #

distorOrdPrc.resFees: None. No fees are applicable for obtaining the data set.

dataIdInfo.idAbs: This feature class/shapefile contains locations of Hospitals for 50 US states, Washington D.C., US territories of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, Northern Mariana Islands, Palau, and Virgin Islands. The dataset only includes hospital facilities based on data acquired from various state departments or federal sources which has been referenced in the SOURCE field. Hospital facilities which do not occur in these sources will be not present in the database. The source data was available in a variety of formats (pdfs, tables, webpages, etc.) which was cleaned and geocoded and then converted into a spatial database. The database does not contain nursing homes or health centers. Hospitals have been categorized into children, chronic disease, critical access, general acute care, long term care, military, psychiatric, rehabilitation, special, and women based on the range of the available values from the various sources after removing similarities.
dataIdInfo.idPurp: This feature class/shapefile contains Hospitals derived from various sources (refer SOURCE field) for the Homeland Infrastructure Foundation-Level Data (HIFLD) database. (https://gii.dhs.gov/HIFLD)
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL); Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Geospatial Management Office (GMO) .

Consts.useLimit: None (Public Use). Users are advised to read the data set's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations.

LegConsts.useLimit: Distributor assumes no liability for misuse of data.

dataExt.exDesc: Time period date range corresponds to the data range covered by the SOURCEDATE field

dataIdInfo.suppInfo: This feature class/shapefile has a one-to-many relationship class (HospitalsToTrauma)/relate with the “Trauma_Levels” table. This table captures the relationship between Hospitals and the state trauma level designations. “Hospitals” is the origin using STATE as the primary key. “Trauma_Levels” table is the destination using STATE as the foreign key.

report.measDesc: No formal attribute accuracy test was conducted. Data from the authoritative sources (as identified in other sections of this metadata) is assumed to be correct and accurate. See also “Overview Description” in the “Entities and Attributes” metadata section.

Data were extracted from sources (identified in the SOURCE field) and other open source websites to populate the attribute fields. For string and date data types, NOT AVAILABLE values donate missing data that was not present in the source data. For numeric data types, -999 denotes missing data that was not present in the source data. During quality control, attributes were checked to ensure that they fell within the expected ranges or had expected/valid values. Text attribute values were checked for spelling and usage consistency. No topology tests were conducted. All records were verified to occur within the spatial domain of the feature class/shapefile.
Data is complete as of the most recent date indicated in the Time Period section.

None; all efforts are made to place the geographic representation of the facility on-entity using the best available imagery from a wide variety of sources. Users should read and understand the VAL_METHOD metadata as well as the Processing Steps.
None; dataset contains no vertical data.

prcStep.stepDesc: Authoritative data sources are identified through open source research. That data is then acquired and transformed into a usable data format. The properly formatted data then goes through preprocessing steps that includes a geocoding operation to give spatial context to the data. The data goes through further refinement using remote sensing techniques. After preprocessing, every record goes through a review process and each point is moved to a facility based on-entity location based on interpretation of the best available imagery and open source information. Hospitals with multi-building campus were located on a large building towards the center of campus. The VAL_DATE field indicates the date in which the review step took place.
